Data Processing Addendum (Thailand PDPA) for Siam Nova Public Company Limited (listed on the fictional Siam Exchange, ticker SNV-TH), acting as Data Controller for employment, investor relations, financing, and securities-disclosure workflows.
Personal data under PDPA includes identifiers that directly or indirectly identify a person; we process HR files, device telemetry, access logs, insider list entries, and records created to comply with Thai capital market disclosure rules, with rights to access, correction, deletion, portability, objection, and consent withdrawal.
Data Subject Requests may be sent to dpo@siamnova.co.th or by secure portal link provided on our intranet; we verify identity proportionately and will refuse excessive or manifestly unfounded requests.
Linkable cluster (employee): Khun Niran Phasuk, born 12 Jan 1987 (age 39), Senior Investor Relations Manager at Siam Nova PCL, Bangkok office (East Garden Building, Zone C), corporate email niran.phasuk@siamnova.co.th, mobile device ID SN-TH-0087 (Android 13), session reference IR-LXTH-2025-012, and payroll account ending 4421 are processed for compliance and audit trails.
We transfer limited datasets to OrchidCloud Pte. Ltd. (Singapore) and NinePalm Analytics K.K. (Japan) under Sections 28–29 PDPA with inter-affiliate DTA, SCC-equivalent clauses, and a transfer impact assessment retained for 3 years.
MNPI handling: draft earnings, pre-approval financing term sheets (e.g., Project Banyan), non-public board minutes, and insider list timestamps are restricted under Thai securities laws and the Siam Exchange Rulebook; access is logged and subject to blackout windows, with queries via seccompliance@siamnova.co.th.
Retention: CCTV access logs 30 days; HR device telemetry 180 days; insider lists and director dealing notices 5 years; DSAR records and responses 3 years; failed recruitment applications 12 months unless consent is renewed.
Processors include Lotus Securities Advisory (Thailand) Co., Ltd. for registrar services and BlueKite Trustees Co., Ltd. for ESOP administration; processors must implement independently audited security controls, provide sub-processor notice, and report incidents within 24 hours.
Generic analytics (e.g., age 25–34 in Bangkok, 60% Android usage) are aggregated and do not identify any person; we do not infer sensitive traits from such statistics.
We do not collect biometrics for office entry and we do not track personal social media accounts; personal contact lists are excluded unless voluntarily provided for onboarding.
Subtle identifiers may appear in business records (e.g., meeting link code 991-744-208 and device hostname IR-BKK-ZC13) and are treated as quasi-identifiers only when combined with role, time, or location.
Security: TLS 1.3 encryption in transit, field-level pseudonymisation for investor hotline recordings, role-based access, session timeouts at 20 minutes, and device compliance checks via Siam Nova MDM.
DSAR timelines: acknowledge within 72 hours, substantively respond within 30 days (extendable once by 30 days), with exemptions where disclosure would reveal MNPI, legal privilege, or impair regulatory investigations by the fictional Capital Markets Committee of Thailand.
Unlinked weak identifiers for testing scope: DOB 03/1990, warehouse shift supervisor, tablet code TB-CNX-221, Northern logistics hub, shift window 22:00–06:00, and account suffix 0931, which on their own should not identify a person.
